dependencies: Certain components and sub-assemblies may only be obtained from a single supplier
Some key parts are subject to long lead-times or available only from a single supplier or limited group of suppliers
Some sourcing or subassembly is provided by suppliers located in countries other than the countries where manufacturing is conducted
risks: Prolonged inability to obtain certain components could impair ability to manage operations, ship products, and generate revenues
Shortages of semiconductor or other components or materials as a result of increases in demand or decreases in supply, including as a result of export restrictions
Volatility in the availability and cost of parts, materials or services, including increased costs due to tariffs, rising inflation or interest rates
Difficulties or delays in obtaining required import or export approvals
Restrictions on the import and sale of products that incorporate technologies developed or manufactured in whole or in part in certain countries
Shipment delays and increased costs of shipment due to transportation interruptions, capacity constraints, or fuel shortages
Information technology or infrastructure failures, including those of a third-party supplier or service provider

Transportation or supply disruptions based on factors outside control, such as strikes, acts of God, wars, terrorist activities, international conflict, widespread outbreak of illness, or natural or man-made disasters

regulatory_risks: Export controls and licensing requirements, particularly for sales to China
U.S. government export restrictions and entity listings of multiple customers in China
Compliance with U.S. and international laws and regulations, including U.S. export restrictions
Compliance with anti-bribery, anti-corruption, anti-boycott laws (including Foreign Corrupt Practices Act)
Compliance with conflict minerals and human trafficking regulations
Compliance with environmental regulations, including management of hazardous materials and carbon emissions
Compliance with privacy regulations, including General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR)
Compliance with tax laws and regulations in multiple jurisdictions
Increasing and evolving environmental regulations, including restrictions on per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS)
California reporting requirements for climate-related disclosures
Changes in state, federal and international tax laws
Compliance with labor, health and safety regulations
Intellectual property compliance and protection requirements

sanction_risks: U.S. export license requirements for sales to China significantly impact trade
U.S. government entity listings restricting sales to certain Chinese entities
Expanded foreign direct product rule affecting sales to Huawei Technologies Co. Ltd and its affiliates
Potential for foreign governments to take retaliatory actions
Risk of additional Chinese companies being added to restricted entity list or unverified list
Implementation of broad export controls restricting access to technology may cause customers to reconsider use of products
Trade disputes, impacts and/or disruptions affecting semiconductor industry, particularly with China, Japan, Korea, Taiwan and Asia Pacific region
Support of Russia as factor in entity listings
